g.data-narration: Welcome to Advanced Java String Methods! Let's explore powerful text manipulation techniques.
String manipulation is essential for data processing, validation, and formatting in real-world Java applications.
g.text: Strings are used everywhere: user input, file processing, data validation, API responses.
Master these methods to become proficient in text processing and data manipulation.

g.data-narration: This diagram shows key string methods we'll explore: trim, strip, split, replace, format, and more.
trim() removes ASCII whitespace only. strip() (Java 11+) handles Unicode whitespace like non-breaking spaces.
substring() extracts portions of strings using start and end indices. The end index is exclusive.
split() divides strings into arrays using regex delimiters. Watch out for special regex characters!
replace() does literal replacement while replaceAll() uses regex. Use replaceAll() for patterns.
g.text: Regex replacement (l+ means one or more 'l')
💡 Note: replaceFirst() replaces only the first match using regex

g.data-narration: matches() validates strings against regex patterns. Essential for email, phone, and form validation.
format() creates formatted strings using specifiers like %s for strings, %d for integers, %.2f for decimals.
Additional methods: concat() joins strings, join() combines with delimiter, isEmpty()/isBlank() check content.
The example shows contains() checking if a substring exists within a string, returning boolean true or false.
Choose trim() for ASCII, strip() for Unicode. Use replace() for literals, replaceAll() for regex patterns.
Key takeaway: Use strip() for Unicode whitespace, split() with escaped regex, replace() for literals, matches() for validation.

g.text: strip() handles Unicode whitespace (Java 11+), trim() is ASCII only
split() uses regex - escape special characters like . with \\.
replace() = literal, replaceAll() = regex - choose based on your need
matches() validates entire string against regex pattern

g.data-narration: Practice combining methods for complex text processing. Master these to become proficient in Java string manipulation!
g.text: Combine multiple methods to solve complex text processing problems. Practice regularly!
